TEN, and


"Fourth-The number of horse
power that would be available for the
purpose of operating an electric light
and power plant.


And the board of public works is
further directed to furnish at said
time an estimate of the cost of MAX
ing the power plant to be erected as
provided for in the plans for addi-
lions to the water system of sufficient
size and equipping the same with the
additional machinery necessary for
the operation of an electric lighting
plant for the city"


A saving of thousands of dollars
the first year is assured by the plan
and the extra horse power can be
conserved at the up-river water works
and used to run the plant, thus in
surfing a comparatively small cost or
maintenance after the first cost OF
installation, which latter will GER
tainly be saved within short time.
